Paragraph

The integrals of a sequence of functions \(\{ c_{k}\}\) in \(C[a, b]\) are said to be uniformly absolutely continuous on \([a, b]\) if for any \(\epsilon > 0\text{,}\) there exists a \(\delta > 0\) such that for any open subset \(G\) of \([a, b]\) with its length \(|G| < \delta\text{,}\)
\begin{equation*} \int_{G} |c_{k}(x)|^{p}\, dx < \epsilon \text{ for all } k. \end{equation*}
in-context
